user_stories.goal: Enter destination, trip length, budget level, and interests to generate a personalised travel itinerary
user_stories.benefit: Quickly get a tailored plan that matches my time, budget and interests without manual research
acceptance_criteria: A form or JSON input accepts destination, trip_length (days), budget_level (low/medium/high), and a list of interests
Input validation returns clear errors for missing/invalid fields (e.g., non-positive trip_length, unsupported destination) with HTTP 400 for API
Default behaviour: empty interests returns a general curated itinerary; trip_length capped at a configurable max (e.g., 30 days) with a warning
Successful submission returns a generated itinerary payload or redirects to the itinerary view within 5 seconds under normal load

user_stories.goal: Receive a clear day-by-day itinerary for the specified trip length
user_stories.benefit: Understand what to do each day so I can follow the plan and manage my time
acceptance_criteria: Response includes exactly N day entries where N equals trip_length
Each day entry contains at least: a title, ordered activities (morning/afternoon/evening or time ranges), estimated duration, location and brief description
Activities include estimated travel times between locations and a daily summary (total estimated time and cost)
Itinerary is free of duplicate activities on the same day unless explicitly suggested as a repeat

user_stories.goal: Have the itinerary respect my budget level and interests
user_stories.benefit: Ensure recommendations are affordable and aligned with my preferences
acceptance_criteria: Activities are tagged with cost tiers and the system filters or prioritises options consistent with the selected budget_level
Interests are used to prioritise and weight suggestions so at least 60% of activities match at least one selected interest
The itinerary shows an estimated total trip cost and per-day cost breakdown based on chosen activities
When budget or interests are changed and regenerated, the itinerary updates accordingly and differences are highlighted

user_stories.goal: Access itinerary generation via a documented Flask API endpoint
user_stories.benefit: Allow programmatic integration and automation of itinerary generation
acceptance_criteria: Provide a POST /api/itineraries endpoint that accepts JSON input: {destination, trip_length, budget_level, interests}
Successful requests return HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing the itinerary structured by days and activities; validation errors return HTTP 400 with error details
API responses include consistent schema (days array, activity fields) and appropriate HTTP caching headers for identical repeated requests
Rate limiting and basic input size protection are enforced and result in HTTP 429 or 413 as appropriate

user_stories.goal: View, export and share the generated itinerary through a website
user_stories.benefit: Easily read, save and distribute my travel plan from a friendly UI
acceptance_criteria: Website displays the day-by-day itinerary in a readable layout (desktop and mobile responsive) with expand/collapse for day details
Users can export the itinerary as printable PDF and download machine-readable JSON from the UI
Users can get a sharable link to the itinerary (read-only) that loads the same itinerary without requiring re-generation
The UI allows minor edits (reorder or remove activities) and re-calculates daily summaries and total cost immediately
